segments.text: To start with some opening remarks, keeping them short,
one to two minutes, and then we will, um,
have questions for members. Thank you.
So Tina, I'm Denise Bates
and I'm the chair of the Real Estate Authority Board.
I'm here today with Belinda Moffit, uh,
r e's Chief Executive.
Uh, and we are going to both be presenting the annual
review briefing about 20, 23, 24 year.
I've prepared some opening remarks, provide an overview of
that year, uh,
and to comment on areas that, uh, we, the, that
of focus that we have is real estate, uh,
real estate conduct regulator.
So this will take about 10 minutes, um,
because I think, you know, if you wanna ask questions,
we need to leave the room for them. Yeah.
So, um, I think, um, if you keep them really short,
I would say if there's lots to cover,
maybe max like three, two to three minutes.
So then you can cover rest in the, in, uh,
Answer questions. So you'd like me to
Keep your opening remarks brief,
so if you take up 10 minutes, there won't be enough time
for members, uh, for questions.
So if you keep them to three minutes or so, three
or four minutes, that should be sufficient.
Um, alright. Thank you.
I'll do my best to, uh, to do it that way.
So, um, you
presumably know
that we're a Crown agent established under the act,
and that our purpose is to protect the interests
of consumers engaging in real estate transactions
and to promote public confidence in the profession.
And so we carry out our re regulatory
responsibilities in a number of ways.
Uh, we, we, we do the licensing work.
We oversee the code of conduct.
Uh, we provide guidance and education to the profession.
Uh, we deal about, we deal with the whole complaint system,
uh, so that, uh, we can hold licensees
to account for, for, for, uh, for
bad behavior essentially.
And lastly, uh, we are there to protect
and promote the interests of consumers being a consumer
agency, consumer protection agency, as well
as a conduct regulator.
So, two strengths to what we do.
Um, we've got a very good website called Settled, uh,
and that, uh, that is a very good tool for, uh,
prospective purchases to use.
So we do our best to promote that
the year we're talking about 2324.
Uh, it was notable for a number of reasons, um,
probably don't need to tell you that the economic conditions
are challenging, uh, have been since that year.
And that slower market puts a lot
of pressure on the, a the, uh, agents
and the consumers.
So while the residential stocks are increased,
the sales are slow.
And, uh, we have to, uh, the, the
profession has to manage consumer expectations,
uh, and that that can be quite
an recalling market.
So what that's meant for us, actually is a very large
increase in the number of complaints
that we've had to manage.
So I will give you a few numbers.
Uh, 361 complaints, uh,
in the, the year we're looking at, uh, compared with
252, uh, in the 22 3 year.
And, uh, the, the, the, they're continuing
to increase at a pace.
Um, we saw in the year we are looking at, uh,
cases involving some complex issues.
Uh, so that all goes
to slow down the process.
So one of our big initiatives has been
to, um, review our complaints
process, uh, to see if we can streamline it and refine it
and become more efficient.
And we, uh, have done a,
a good job of doing that.
Um, unfortunately, uh, what, what it's,
we are coming up against a wall of a huge wall
of complaints at the time, same time as we are trying to get
through, um, a bit of a backlog.
So that's, uh, and I think we are rising to the challenge,
but, uh, look, the, the good news is the, we, we know that
this complaint environment is probably going to be around,
and so we call it the new normal.
And we'll probably, uh, it'll probably change at some point,
but right now that we, we are dealing with a, a lot
of disappointed people, I'd say.
Um, so just to give you a,
uh, we, we've, we've actually getting
through more complaints
and we are also reducing the time it takes to do them.
Uh, so just to give you some figures, um,
the, the year we're looking at,
we did 3 11 3, 311 complaints.

And this year we've done 487, um,
which I think shows our, uh,
increase in efficiencies given the volume keeps coming in.

complaint environment, which is quite difficult
and, you know, puts pressure on people that are doing it.
So you expect, you might expect that that, uh,
licensing numbers would reduced a lot in the environment.
Well, in that year, uh, reduced by only 2.9%.

1,710 new licensees joining.
And this year, uh, the number has increased again, uh,
the overall number to 15,692.

to 72% following

if it doesn't now Belinda, you can take that. I think,

to complete the CAC process within nine months.

A atory from Dr. Ham. Thank you.

What's, what's actually drive driving that?

source_metadata.description:
Review briefing on the 2023/24 annual review of the Real Estate Agents Authority
Real Estate Agents Authority
Belinda Moffat, Chief Executive/Registrar
Denese Bates KC, REA Board Chair
